schemas.title: Study Identity & Discovery
schemas.description: Make the study discoverable and uniquely identifiable; provide the minimum context needed to understand “what this is” and relate it to other studies/collections.
schemas.name: tier1.studyIdentityDiscovery

fields.name: studyID
fields.description: The stable, unique identifier for a study that never changes, even if other study metadata changes (like the study name, short name, owner, or project). Example: STUDY_SCA

fields.name: cohortID
fields.description: Unique identifier for the cohort

fields.name: cohortName
fields.description: Name of cohort

fields.name: dataSubtype
fields.description: Further qualification of dataType, which may be used to indicate the state of processing of the data, aggregation of the data, or presence of metadata.

fields.name: dataType
fields.description: Types of input/output data in bioinformatics pipelines

fields.name: grantNumber
fields.description: Grant number including activity code, institute code, and serial number (e.g. U01MH103392)

fields.name: institution
fields.description: Institution conducting study

fields.name: longitudinalStudyID
fields.description: Linked longitudinal study identifier

fields.name: metadataCoverageNotes
fields.description: Free-text clarification of what is included/excluded in the metadata record and why (e.g., “some measures collected but not shareable due to licensing”).

fields.name: metadataCoverageScope
fields.description: Indicates whether the archived metadata describes all data collected in the study or only the data types made available for external access.

fields.name: metadataFileID
fields.description: Identifies a specific metadata artifact or file (i.e, a particular "measures.tsv" version, etc. If the file is uploaded to Synapse, this will be the Synapse file entity ID. Examples: syn99887766 (or FILE_STUDY_SCA_measures_v2)

fields.name: metadataType
fields.description: For files of dataSubtype: metadata, a description of the type of metadata in the file.

fields.name: principalInvestigator
fields.description: Name(s) of PI

fields.name: principalInvestigatorContact
fields.description: Email of (each) PI

fields.name: principalInvestigatorRoles
fields.description: Distinguishes original vs current vs co-PI

fields.name: studyAbstract
fields.description: The short study description/abstract used to describe the study that is visible on the Portal study card.

fields.name: studyDescription
fields.description: The synID of the wiki that contains the study description.Description of the study, including the types of experimental assays, model systems, types of analysis, description of cohort, associated vulnerable populations, special categories of data, rare diseases, etc. Maps to DUOplus2.

fields.name: studyKey
fields.description: The short acronym for a project the study is associated with.

fields.name: studyMetadata
fields.description: The wiki from the CLASSIC Portal - backend that describes study metadata files

fields.name: studyName
fields.description: Name of the study

fields.name: studyProjectIdentifier
fields.description: The Synapse Project identifier (synID) with which this Study is related.

schemas.title: Governance, Access & Provenance
schemas.description: Let users understand whether and how the dataset can be accessed and reused (policy + operational access steps), without needing back-and-forth.
schemas.name: tier2.governanceAccessProvenance

fields.name: studyID
fields.description: The stable, unique identifier for a study that never changes, even if other study metadata changes (like the study name, short name, owner, or project). Example: STUDY_SCA

fields.name: accessContact
fields.description: Point of contact (team, email alias, or role) for access questions.

fields.name: accessProcedure
fields.description: Brief description of the steps required to request and obtain access to the dataset.

fields.name: accessRequestURL
fields.description: URL to the access request page/portal or repository instructions for obtaining access.

fields.name: accessRequirements
fields.description: Checklist of materials required for access (e.g., DUA, IRB letter, training).

fields.name: accessRestrictions
fields.description: Access level (ex., open, controlled, embargoed)

fields.name: accessReviewBody
fields.description: The entity that reviews/approves access requests (e.g., DAC, IRB, sponsor).

fields.name: aggregationLevel
fields.description: Specifies whether a metadata value represents individual-level data or an aggregate summary

fields.name: appliesTo
fields.description: Indicates the level of entity to which a metadata value applies (e.g., individual participant or population summary).

fields.name: cohortID
fields.description: Unique identifier for the cohort

fields.name: consentType
fields.description: Consent type (broad, tiered, etc.)

fields.name: dataLocation
fields.description: Where the dataset is hosted or distributed (e.g., Synapse project, NIMH/NDAR collection, dbGaP).

fields.name: dataUseConditions
fields.description: Data use conditions

fields.name: deidentificationMethod
fields.description: De-ID method applied

fields.name: externallyRequestable
fields.description: Availability indicator to request the data.

fields.name: irbApprovalDescription
fields.description: Free-text description providing additional context about IRB approval, exemption, or oversight.

fields.name: irbApprovalNumber
fields.description: The identifier assigned by the IRB for an approved or reviewed study, if applicable.

fields.name: irbExemptionType
fields.description: The regulatory exemption category under which the study was determined to be exempt from IRB review.

fields.name: irbInstitution
fields.description: The institution or ethics committee responsible for IRB review or determination.

fields.name: irbScope
fields.description: Scope of IRB in relation to the study.

fields.name: irbStatus
fields.description: The regulatory determination of IRB oversight for the study.

fields.name: optional persistent row identifier
fields.description: Use studyID as the anchor, plus a persistent row identifier to identify a specific file/version, etc.

fields.name: optional persistent row identifier  - value
fields.description: Enter a unique identifier for the selection made.

fields.name: populationScope
fields.description: Identifies the population described by the metadata (e.g., study-wide, cohort, or study arm).

schemas.title: Cohort & Population
schemas.description: Describe who is in the study and how the cohort was defined/ascertained, enough for a user to judge relevance before requesting access.
schemas.name: tier3.cohortPopulation

fields.name: studyID
fields.description: The stable, unique identifier for a study that never changes, even if other study metadata changes (like the study name, short name, owner, or project). Example: STUDY_SCA

fields.name: ageGroup
fields.description: Broad age-based population grouping represented in the cohort.

fields.name: aggregationLevel
fields.description: Specifies whether a metadata value represents individual-level data or an aggregate summary

fields.name: appliesTo
fields.description: Indicates the level of entity to which a metadata value applies (e.g., individual participant or population summary).

fields.name: ascertainmentContext
fields.description: Where participants came from: the categories describe sampling context, independent of disease focus

fields.name: constructDomain
fields.description: Domain of measured construct

fields.name: criteriaSelectionIntensity
fields.description: Overall restrictiveness of inclusion/exclusion criteria.

fields.name: diseaseFocus
fields.description: Primary disease or condition focus of the study. A categorical descriptor summarizing participant health status selection and ascertainment context for a study cohort, intended to support discovery and comparison across studies.

fields.name: eligibilityCriteriaSummary
fields.description: A brief summary of the major inclusion and exclusion criteria that determined who could enroll in the study/cohort.

fields.name: eligibilityCriteriaURL
fields.description: Link to the protocol, registry record, or document containing full inclusion/exclusion criteria.

fields.name: ethnicity
fields.description: Self-reported race/ethnicity. A self-identified cultural classification that reflects whether a participant identifies as being of Hispanic or Latino origin, independent of race.

fields.name: ethnicityDistribution
fields.description: A summary of the proportion or count of participants in a population by self-identified ethnicity.

fields.name: exclusionTags
fields.description: High-level exclusion characteristics used to restrict enrollment.

fields.name: externalParticipantID
fields.description: Other external identifier, if no GUID exists

fields.name: gender
fields.description: The socially and culturally constructed identity that describes a person’s self-identified gender, which may or may not correspond to sex assigned at birth and may change over time.

fields.name: genderDistribution
fields.description: A summary of the proportion or count of participants in a population by self-identified gender.

fields.name: generation
fields.description: Generation within family cohort (ex., F0=original, F1=child, F2=grandchild). Indicates the generational position of participants within a family-based cohort, relative to the originally enrolled participant(s).

fields.name: GUID
fields.description: Global Unique Identifier: unique across studies and repositories. If a participant has been assigned a global identifier (e.g., NDAR GUID), include it here to support linkage across studies and repositories. Otherwise, leave this field blank.

fields.name: inclusionTags
fields.description: High-level inclusion characteristics used to define the enrolled population.

fields.name: optional persistent row identifier
fields.description: Use studyID as the anchor, plus a persistent row identifier to identify a specific file/version, etc.

fields.name: optional persistent row identifier  - value
fields.description: Enter a unique identifier for the selection made.

fields.name: populationDescriptor
fields.description: Free-text descriptor capturing key population characteristics not covered by controlled terms (e.g., “male veterans”, “older adults with caregiver support”).

fields.name: populationScope
fields.description: Identifies the population described by the metadata (e.g., study-wide, cohort, or study arm).

fields.name: populationType
fields.description: Broad classification of the population sampled in the study/cohort.

fields.name: primaryHealthFocus
fields.description: Why participants were included: these categories describe participant health status at enrollment, not outcomes.

fields.name: race
fields.description: Self-reported race/ethnicity. A socially defined classification that reflects a participant’s self-identified racial group(s), based on shared social, historical, or cultural experiences, and not biological or genetic differences.
enum: Native Hawaiian or Other Pacific Islander

fields.name: raceDistribution
fields.description: A summary of the proportion or count of participants in a population by self-identified race category or categories.

fields.name: relationToOriginal
fields.description: Relationship to original participant (ex., self, child, grandchild, spouse). Describes the familial relationship(s) of participants to the initially enrolled participant(s) in a family-based cohort.

fields.name: selectionIntensity
fields.description: How restrictive enrollment was: the categories describe selection methodology

fields.name: sex
fields.description: The biological classification of a participant, typically assigned at birth based on physical and genetic characteristics.

fields.name: sexDistribution
fields.description: A summary of the proportion or count of participants in a population by sex category.

fields.name: sexEligible
fields.description: Sex eligibility criteria for cohort inclusion (if the study restricts enrollment).

schemas.title: Study Design & Time
schemas.description: Describe when and how often data were collected (longitudinal structure, bursts, waves, follow-up intervals, timestamp availability). This is key for interpretation and longitudinal/APC use cases.
schemas.name: tier4.studyDesignTime

fields.name: studyID
fields.description: The stable, unique identifier for a study that never changes, even if other study metadata changes (like the study name, short name, owner, or project). Example: STUDY_SCA

fields.name: administrationContext
fields.description: The physical or situational setting in which a data collection activity or assessment is administered. Describes where data collection occurs, not how, who, or what. Intended for general or summary-level reporting, not per-participant metadata.

fields.name: administrationMode
fields.description: The manner in which a digital assessment or data collection activity is administered to participants.

fields.name: aggregationLevel
fields.description: Specifies whether a metadata value represents individual-level data or an aggregate summary

fields.name: appliesTo
fields.description: Indicates the level of entity to which a metadata value applies (e.g., individual participant or population summary).

fields.name: burstDesign
fields.description: A summary description of the study’s burst-based sampling structure (bursts, timing, follow-up cadence, and recruitment waves).

fields.name: burstFrequency
fields.description: The planned frequency at which bursts occur across the study timeline.

fields.name: burstLength
fields.description: The planned duration of each burst period of intensive measurement.

fields.name: collectionModality
fields.description: The primary mode by which data were collected for a variable or assessment.

fields.name: endYear
fields.description: Latest calendar year of data collection

fields.name: enrollmentAge
fields.description: The age or ages of a participant at the time of enrollment or at defined enrollment-related timepoints in a study, including single ages, age ranges, or multiple ages associated with longitudinal or staged enrollment.

fields.name: enrollmentTimepoint
fields.description: A named study milestone at which enrollment occurs (e.g., baseline, re-enrollment, extension phase).

fields.name: followUpInterval
fields.description: The planned amount of time between participant follow-up assessments, visits, or measurement periods.

fields.name: followUpYear
fields.description: Years since original cohort enrollment. Summarizes the amount of time elapsed since original cohort enrollment for which participant data are available. Values can be in: minYears, maxYears, years.

fields.name: followUpYearDescription
fields.description: Free-text clarification (optional)

fields.name: hasObservationTimestamp
fields.description: Indicates whether individual observations/records include a date or timestamp of data collection.

fields.name: numberOfBursts
fields.description: The number of distinct burst periods of intensive measurement scheduled per participant.

fields.name: optional persistent row identifier
fields.description: Use studyID as the anchor, plus a persistent row identifier to identify a specific file/version, etc.

fields.name: optional persistent row identifier  - value
fields.description: Enter a unique identifier for the selection made.

fields.name: populationScope
fields.description: Identifies the population described by the metadata (e.g., study-wide, cohort, or study arm).

fields.name: promptFrequency
fields.description: Frequency of prompts. Valid values: free text, OR Multiple, Varies, Unknown

fields.name: promptingScheduleType
fields.description: EMA schedule type. Valid values: free text, OR Multiple, Varies, Unknown

fields.name: recruitmentWave
fields.description: Enrollment wave (baseline, follow-up, etc.). Identifies the recruitment phase or wave during which participants were enrolled in a study, reflecting distinct enrollment periods defined by study design or operational milestones.

fields.name: responseWindowMinutes
fields.description: Response window (minutes). Valid values: free text, OR Multiple, Varies, Unknown.

fields.name: rollingEnrollment
fields.description: Indicates whether participant enrollment occurs continuously over time rather than within a fixed recruitment window.

fields.name: seasons
fields.description: Seasons during which data were collected

fields.name: startYear
fields.description: Earliest calendar year of data collection

fields.name: studyArmID
fields.description: A stable identifier for a defined study arm, group, or condition assignment used to structure participant enrollment, intervention, or comparison. Examples: ARM:CONTROL, ARM:TREATMENT_A, ARM:HIGH_RISK, ARM:CASE, ARM:COMPARATOR

fields.name: studyComponentID
fields.description: A stable identifier for a defined component of a study (e.g., wave, arm, period) used to scope metadata summaries.

fields.name: studyComponentType
fields.description: The category of study subcomponent identified by studyComponentID (e.g., wave, arm, period), used to indicate what kind of study structure the component represents for scoping metadata summaries.

fields.name: studyPeriodID
fields.description: A stable identifier for a defined time period during which data collection occurred under consistent procedures or conditions. Examples: PERIOD:BASELINE, PERIOD:FOLLOWUP_6MO, PERIOD:COVID_ERA, PERIOD:2020_2021

fields.name: studyStartYear
fields.description: The calendar year in which study recruitment or data collection began. Use "Unknown" if not available.

fields.name: studyWaveID
fields.description: A stable identifier for a defined recruitment wave or enrollment phase within a study. Examples: WAVE:1, WAVE:2, WAVE:PILOT, WAVE:EXTENSION

fields.name: studyWaves
fields.description: The recruitment or enrollment wave(s) represented in the study population.

fields.name: temporalCoverage
fields.description: The time period or periods during which data were collected for a study, cohort, or variable, summarized at an aggregate level.

fields.name: temporalCoverageDescription
fields.description: Free-text clarification (optional)

fields.name: years
fields.description: Explicit list of years, if non-continuous. Four-digit integers (e.g., 2019)

schemas.title: Measures, Instruments, Derived Scores, Variables & Availability Summaries
schemas.description: Describe what was measured (constructs, instrument identity, administration modality/technology) at a level that supports discovery and basic interpretability. Make derived outputs interpretable (what a score/variable represents, and its general derivation form), especially when item/trial-level data aren’t shared. Help users answer “is there enough data for my question?” without accessing participant-level files, counts, ranges, units, or timepoint-scoped summaries.
schemas.name: tier5.measuresInstruments

fields.name: studyID
fields.description: The stable, unique identifier for a study that never changes, even if other study metadata changes (like the study name, short name, owner, or project). Example: STUDY_SCA

fields.name: administrationInstructions
fields.description: The instructions provided to participants describing exactly how to perform the instrument/task (verbatim or summarized).

fields.name: administrationMode
fields.description: The manner in which a digital assessment or data collection activity is administered to participants.

fields.name: administrationProcedure
fields.description: The source of the implementation (e.g., NIH Toolbox, PEBL, PsychoPy script, custom app).

fields.name: aggregationLevel
fields.description: Specifies whether a metadata value represents individual-level data or an aggregate summary

fields.name: appliesTo
fields.description: Indicates the level of entity to which a metadata value applies (e.g., individual participant or population summary).

fields.name: appPlatform
fields.description: App or platform used. Valid values: free text OR Multiple, Varies, Unknown.

fields.name: constructDescription
fields.description: One-sentence description of how the construct was operationalized in the study.

fields.name: dataAvailabilitySummary
fields.description: An aggregate description of the amount and demographic composition of available data for a study, cohort, or variable, provided without individual-level records.

fields.name: derivationMethod
fields.description: A high-level description of how a score was derived from raw or intermediate data.

fields.name: derivedScore
fields.description: A summary description of a score derived from a measure, including how it was calculated.

fields.name: derivedVariableName
fields.description: The name of a derived variable or score as it appears in the dataset or metadata record.

fields.name: derivedVariableType
fields.description: A controlled category describing the general method or type of derivation used to compute a derived variable or score (e.g., sum score, composite score, standardized score).

fields.name: deviceType
fields.description: The type of digital device used for data collection, applicable only when data are collected digitally.

fields.name: inputMethod
fields.description: The primary method by which participant input or data capture occurs during digital data collection.

fields.name: instrumentCitation
fields.description: The primary citation(s) describing the instrument/task implementation used in the study (e.g., published paper, manual, DOI).

fields.name: instrumentID
fields.description: a stable identifier used to reference a specific instrument, measure, or assessment used in a study (e.g., a validated questionnaire, cognitive task, interview protocol, or sensor-based assessment).

fields.name: instrumentName
fields.description: Name of the instrument or scale used (e.g., PHQ-9, NIH Toolbox).

fields.name: instrumentSource
fields.description: Where the instrument originated

fields.name: instrumentVersion
fields.description: Version identifier for the instrument implementation used (e.g., vendor version, protocol revision, “v2”, “2016 revision”).

fields.name: isValidatedMeasure
fields.description: Indicates whether a measure or instrument used to assess a construct has been previously validated.

fields.name: manufacturerModel
fields.description: Device make/model. Valid values: free text OR Multiple, Varies, Unknown.

fields.name: measuredConstruct
fields.description: A conceptual domain or construct that a study collected data on (e.g., depression, working memory, physical activity).

fields.name: measureDescription
fields.description: A brief description of how a measure was administered and what it was designed to assess.

fields.name: measureID
fields.description: Persistent per-measure row identifier. (ex., "MEAS_SCA_STROOP_v1")

fields.name: measureType
fields.description: The general type of measure used to assess a construct.

fields.name: operatingSystem
fields.description: OS and version. Valid values: free text OR Multiple, Varies, Unknown.

fields.name: optional persistent row identifier
fields.description: Use studyID as the anchor, plus a persistent row identifier to identify a specific file/version, etc.

fields.name: optional persistent row identifier  - value
fields.description: Enter a unique identifier for the selection made.

fields.name: populationScope
fields.description: Identifies the population described by the metadata (e.g., study-wide, cohort, or study arm).

fields.name: preprocessingSteps
fields.description: Preprocessing/cleaning applied

fields.name: proportion
fields.description: A numeric value representing the fraction of a population associated with a given category, typically expressed as a percentage or decimal.

fields.name: protocolDescription
fields.description: The synID of the wiki that contains the protocol, study design and goals

fields.name: protocolURL
fields.description: Link to the protocol, manual, script, or documentation describing the instrument implementation.

fields.name: rawOrDerived
fields.description: Raw vs derived data flag

fields.name: scoreName
fields.description: Is the human-readable name of a score derived from a measure, as used or labeled by the study.

fields.name: scoreType
fields.description: Describes the general method or form by which a score was derived from a measure or assessment.

fields.name: sensors
fields.description: Sensors enabled (GPS, accelerometer, etc.). Valid values: free text OR Multiple, Varies, Unknown.

fields.name: softwarePlatform
fields.description: The software environment or application used to deliver, collect, or manage digitally collected data.

fields.name: stimulusResponseMode
fields.description: How participants interacted with the instrument during administration.

fields.name: timepoint
fields.description: A label identifying the study timepoint or phase at which the variable value or summary applies (e.g., baseline, follow-up).

fields.name: totalParticipants
fields.description: The total number of unique participants for whom data are available within the specified study, cohort, or scope.

fields.name: unit
fields.description: The unit of measurement associated with a variable’s values or summary statistics (e.g., years, kg/m²).

fields.name: valueRange
fields.description: The observed minimum and maximum values of a continuous variable within the reported population scope.

fields.name: valueRangeMaxValue
fields.description: The maximum observed value of a continuous variable within the specified population scope.

fields.name: valueRangeMinValue
fields.description: The minimum observed value of a continuous variable within the specified population scope.

fields.name: variableSummary
fields.description: Aggregate summary statistics describing the observed distribution and availability of a variable in a dataset, without exposing participant-level values.
